volumeTitle: The Merchant. The Braggart Soldier. The Ghost. The Persian
label: The Persian (Section 5.2.810b - Section 5.2.857b)

sections.label: Section 5.2.810b
sections.original: Em , serva rúsum .
sections.translation: Hey — be on your guard [*] again .

sections.label: Section 5.2.811
sections.original: Delúde , ut lubet , erus dum hínc abest .
sections.translation: Sport on just as you please , while your master ’ s away from here .

sections.label: Section 5.2.812
sections.original: Viden út tuis dictis páreo ?
sections.translation: Don ’ t you see how obedient I am to your request ? But why , on the other hand , are not you obedient to my request as well , and why don ’ t you do that which I advise you ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.814b
sections.original: Quid ést id ?
sections.translation: What ’ s that ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.815
sections.original: Restím tu tibí cápe crassam ac suspende te .
sections.translation: Do you take a stout rope for yourself , and go hang yourself .

sections.label: Section 5.2.816
sections.original: Cave sís me attigás , ne tibi hóc scipióne
sections.translation: Take you care , will you , that you don ’ t touch me , lest I give you a heavy return with this walking-stick .

sections.label: Section 5.2.817b
sections.original: Vteré , te condóno .
sections.translation: Make use of it ; I give you leave .

sections.label: Section 5.2.818
sections.original: Iam iám , Paegnium , da paúsam .
sections.translation: Come , come , Paegnium , put an end to it .

sections.label: Section 5.2.819
sections.original: Égo pol vos erádicabo .
sections.translation: By my faith , I ’ ll utterly destroy you all .

sections.label: Section 5.2.819b
sections.original: At te ílle , qui suprá nos habitat ,
sections.translation: But he , who dwells above [*] us ,

sections.label: Section 5.2.820
sections.original: qui tibi male volt maleque faciet . non hi dicunt , verum ego .
sections.translation: wishes you all ill , and will do you all ill . It ’ s not they that tell you so , but I .

sections.label: Section 5.2.821
sections.original: Age , circumfer mulsum , bibere da usque plenis cantharis .
sections.translation: Come ( to the SLAVES .) carry round the honied wine [*] ; give us drink in goblets quite full : it ’ s a long time now since we last drank ; we ’ ve been athirst too long .

sections.label: Section 5.2.823
sections.original: Di faciant ut id bibatis quod vos numquam transeat .
sections.translation: May the Gods grant that you may drink that which may never pass through you .

sections.label: Section 5.2.824
sections.original: Nequeo , leno , quin tibi saltem staticulum , olim quem Hegea
sections.translation: I cannot forbear , Procurer , from at least dancing a hornpipe [*] for you , which Hegea formerly

sections.label: Section 5.2.825
sections.original: faciebat . vide vero , si tibi satis placet .
sections.translation: composed . But just look if it quite pleases you .

sections.label: Section 5.2.825b
sections.original: Me quoque volo
sections.translation: I ’ d like also to repeat that one which Diodorus formerly composed in Ionia .

sections.label: Section 5.2.827
sections.original: Malum ego vobis dabo , ni abitis .
sections.translation: I ’ ll be doing you a mischief , if you are not off !

sections.label: Section 5.2.827b
sections.original: Etiam muttis , impudens ?
sections.translation: Still muttering , impudence ? If you provoke me , I ’ ll just now be bringing you the Persian again .

sections.label: Section 5.2.829
sections.original: iam taceo hercle Atque tu Persa es , qui me usque ádmutilavisti ad cutem .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I ’ m silenced now . Why , you are the Persian that has been fleecing me to the quick !

sections.label: Section 5.2.830
sections.original: Tace , stulte : hic eius geminust frater .
sections.translation: Hold your tongue , simpleton ; this is his twin-brother .

sections.label: Section 5.2.830b
sections.original: Hicinest ?
sections.translation: Is it he ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.830c
sections.original: Ac geminissumus .
sections.translation: Aye , and a very twin of twins .

sections.label: Section 5.2.831
sections.original: Di deaeque et te et geminum fratrem excrucient .
sections.translation: May the Gods and Goddesses rack both yourself and your twin-brother .

sections.label: Section 5.2.831b
sections.original: Qui te perdidit :
sections.translation: Him , you mean , who has been ruining you ; for I don ’ t deserve anything .

sections.label: Section 5.2.832b
sections.original: At enim quod ille meruit , tibi id obsit volo .
sections.translation: But still , what he deserves , I hope that that may prove to your undoing .

sections.label: Section 5.2.833
sections.original: Agite sultis , hunc lúdificemus .
sections.translation: Come , if you like , let ’ s have some sport with this fellow ,

sections.label: Section 5.2.833b
sections.original: Nisi si dignust , non opust .
sections.translation: unless he isn ’ t deemed worthy of it .

sections.label: Section 5.2.834
sections.original: et me haud par est .
sections.translation: But it isn ’ t proper for me .

sections.label: Section 5.2.834b
sections.original: Credo eo , quia non ínconciliavit , cúm te emo .
sections.translation: For the reason , I suppose , that he made no difficulties when I purchased you .

sections.label: Section 5.2.835
sections.original: At tamen non — tamen —
sections.translation: But still —

sections.label: Section 5.2.835b
sections.original: Cave ergo sis maló , et sequere me .
sections.translation: No still . Beware , then , of a mishap , will you , and obey me . It becomes you to be heedful of my orders ; for , i ’ faith , had it not been for me and my protection , he would before long have made a street-walker of you . But such are some of those who have gained their freedom , unless they thwart their patron , they don ’ t appear to themselves free enough , or wise enough , or honest enough ,

sections.label: Section 5.2.840
sections.original: ní id effecit , ni ei male dixit , ni grato ingratus repertust .
sections.translation: unless they oppose him , unless they abuse him , unless they are found ungrateful to him who has been kind .

sections.label: Section 5.2.841
sections.original: Pol bene facta tua me hortantur , tuo ut imperio paream .
sections.translation: I ’ troth , your kindnesses command me to pay obedience to your commands .

sections.label: Section 5.2.842
sections.original: Ego sum tibi patronus plane , qui huic pro te argentum dedi .
sections.translation: I clearly am your patron , who paid the money for you to that man ( pointing to DORDALUS .) in return for that , I choose that he shall be made sport of .

sections.label: Section 5.2.843b
sections.original: Meo ego ín loco sedulo cúrabo .
sections.translation: For my part , I ’ ll do my utmost .

sections.label: Section 5.2.844
sections.original: Certo ílli homines mihi néscio quid mali cónsulunt , quod faciánt .
sections.translation: For sure , these persons are consulting to do something , I know not what , to injure me .

sections.label: Section 5.2.844b
sections.original: Heus vos .
sections.translation: Hark you .

sections.label: Section 5.2.845
sections.original: Quid aís ?
sections.translation: What do you say ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.845b
sections.original: Hicin Dordalus ést leno , qui hic liberas virgines mércatur ?
sections.translation: Is this person here , Dordalus the Procurer , who deals in free women ? Is this he who was formerly st stalwart ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.846b
sections.original: Quae haec rés est ? ei , colaphó me icit .
sections.translation: What means this ? ( PAEGNIUM strikes him .) Oh , oh ! he has given me a slap in the face ! I ’ ll do you a mischief .

sections.label: Section 5.2.847b
sections.original: At tibi nós dedimus dabimúsque etiam .
sections.translation: But we have done you one , and shall do it again too .

sections.label: Section 5.2.847c
sections.original: Ei , natis pérvellit .
sections.translation: Oh , oh ! he ’ s pinching my behind .

sections.label: Section 5.2.848
sections.original: Licet : iám diu saepe sunt éxpunctae .
sections.translation: Of course ; it has been many a time twitched before this .

sections.label: Section 5.2.848b
sections.original: Loqueré tu etiam , frustúm pueri ?
sections.translation: Are you still prating , you bit of a boy ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.849
sections.original: Patróne mi , i intro , amábo , ad cenam .
sections.translation: My patron , do , there ’ s a dear , come in-doors to dinner .

sections.label: Section 5.2.850
sections.original: Mea Ignávia , tu nunc me ínrides ?
sections.translation: My lump of laziness , are you now scoffing at me ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.851
sections.original: Quíane te vocó , bene ut tíbi sit ?
sections.translation: What , because I invite you to enjoy yourself ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.851b
sections.original: Nólo mihi bene ésse .
sections.translation: I don ’ t want to enjoy myself .

sections.label: Section 5.2.851c
sections.original: Ne sit .
sections.translation: Then don ’ t .

sections.label: Section 5.2.852
sections.original: Quíd igitur ? sescénti nummi quíd agunt , quas turbás danunt ?
sections.translation: How then ? The six hundred didrachms , how are they ? What disturbances they do cause .

sections.label: Section 5.2.853
sections.original: Mále disperii , scíunt referre próbe inimico grátiam .
sections.translation: I ’ m utterly undone ! They understand full well how to return the compliment to an enemy .

sections.label: Section 5.2.854
sections.original: Satis súmpsimus súpplici iam .
sections.translation: Have we now had satisfaction enough ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.854b
sections.original: Fateór , manus vobís do .
sections.translation: I confess it ; I hold up my hands [*] to you .

sections.label: Section 5.2.855
sections.original: Et póst dabis sub fúrcis .
sections.translation: And , ere long , you shall be holding them beneath the bilboes [*] . Be off in-doors . [*]

sections.label: Section 5.2.856
sections.original: Abi íntro — in crucem .
sections.translation: To perdition !

sections.label: Section 5.2.856b
sections.original: Án me hic parum éxercitum hísce
sections.translation: Have these fellows here worked me in too slight a degree ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.857b
sections.original: Convenísse te Tóxilum me ⋯
sections.translation: Keep in mind that you met with a Toxilus . ( To the AUDIENCE .) Spectators , kindly fare you well . The Procurer is demolished . Grant us your applause .
